docs.text: Kotlin Reffrences This is an alternative set of documentation for Kotlin, designed to be simpler and easier to use than traditional resources. The examples included here are generated with the help of a large language model (LLM). While care has been taken to ensure accuracy, there may still be mistakes. If you find any issues or inaccuracies, please feel free to open a pull request with your corrections. Contributions are always welcome! The source can be find on GitHub

docs.text: This was made by Axel Gard I made this becouse I found the kotlin docs to be a little unclear and I was missing exampels at times. So I made this for my own use. I have used GPT-OSS 20B for the exampels. So Im sure there are some mistakes but I will try and fix them in the case that I find them. If you find them, feel free to make a PR or a Issue .
docs.title: About

docs.text: AutoCloseable Returns an AutoCloseable instance that executes the specified closeAction upon invocation of its close() function. expect inline fun AutoCloseable ( crossinline closeAction : () -> Unit ): AutoCloseable ( source ) import kotlin.AutoCloseable fun main () { val resource = AutoCloseable { println ( "Resource closed" ) } resource . use { println ( "Using the resource" ) } } Source
docs.title: AutoCloseable

docs.text: Returns an AutoCloseable instance that executes the specified closeAction upon invocation of its close() function. expect inline fun AutoCloseable ( crossinline closeAction : () -> Unit ): AutoCloseable ( source ) import kotlin.AutoCloseable fun main () { val resource = AutoCloseable { println ( "Resource closed" ) } resource . use { println ( "Using the resource" ) } } Source
docs.title: AutoCloseable

docs.text: addSuppressed When supported by the platform, adds the specified exception to the list of exceptions that were suppressed in order to deliver this exception. expect fun Throwable . addSuppressed ( exception : Throwable )( source ) fun main () { val primary = RuntimeException ( "Primary exception" ) val suppressed = RuntimeException ( "Suppressed exception" ) try { // Something that throws the primary exception throw primary } catch ( e : Throwable ) { // Add the suppressed exception e . addSuppressed ( suppressed ) // Re‑throw so the stack trace contains both throw e } } Source
docs.title: addSuppressed

docs.text: When supported by the platform, adds the specified exception to the list of exceptions that were suppressed in order to deliver this exception. expect fun Throwable . addSuppressed ( exception : Throwable )( source ) fun main () { val primary = RuntimeException ( "Primary exception" ) val suppressed = RuntimeException ( "Suppressed exception" ) try { // Something that throws the primary exception throw primary } catch ( e : Throwable ) { // Add the suppressed exception e . addSuppressed ( suppressed ) // Re‑throw so the stack trace contains both throw e } } Source
docs.title: addSuppressed

docs.text: also Calls the specified function block with this value as its argument and returns this value. @IgnorableReturnValueinline fun < T > T . also ( block : ( T ) -> Unit ): T ( source ) val numbers = mutableListOf < Int > () . also { list -> list . add ( 1 ) list . add ( 2 ) list . add ( 3 ) } println ( numbers ) // Output: [1, 2, 3] Source
docs.title: also

docs.text: Calls the specified function block with this value as its argument and returns this value. @IgnorableReturnValueinline fun < T > T . also ( block : ( T ) -> Unit ): T ( source ) val numbers = mutableListOf < Int > () . also { list -> list . add ( 1 ) list . add ( 2 ) list . add ( 3 ) } println ( numbers ) // Output: [1, 2, 3] Source
docs.title: also

docs.text: and Performs a bitwise AND operation between the two values. infix inline fun BigInteger . and ( other : BigInteger ): BigInteger ( source ) import java.math.BigInteger fun main () { val a = BigInteger ( "15" ) // binary 1111 val b = BigInteger ( "10" ) // binary 1010 val c = a and b // infix usage of BigInteger.and println ( " $ a AND $ b = $ c " ) // prints: 15 AND 10 = 10 } Source
docs.title: and

docs.text: Performs a bitwise AND operation between the two values. infix inline fun BigInteger . and ( other : BigInteger ): BigInteger ( source ) import java.math.BigInteger fun main () { val a = BigInteger ( "15" ) // binary 1111 val b = BigInteger ( "10" ) // binary 1010 val c = a and b // infix usage of BigInteger.and println ( " $ a AND $ b = $ c " ) // prints: 15 AND 10 = 10 } Source
docs.title: and

docs.text: apply Calls the specified function block with this value as its receiver and returns this value. @IgnorableReturnValueinline fun < T > T . apply ( block : T .() -> Unit ): T ( source ) val numbers = mutableListOf ().apply { add(10) add(20) add(30) } println(numbers) Source
docs.title: apply

docs.text: Calls the specified function block with this value as its receiver and returns this value. @IgnorableReturnValueinline fun < T > T . apply ( block : T .() -> Unit ): T ( source ) val numbers = mutableListOf ().apply { add(10) add(20) add(30) } println(numbers) Source
docs.title: apply

docs.text: arrayOf Returns an array containing the specified elements. expect inline fun < T > arrayOf ( vararg elements : T ): Array < T > ( source ) fun main () { val numbers = arrayOf ( 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ) val names = arrayOf ( "Alice" , "Bob" , "Charlie" ) println ( numbers . joinToString ( ", " )) // 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 println ( names [ 0 ] ) // Alice } Source
docs.title: arrayOf

docs.text: Returns an array containing the specified elements. expect inline fun < T > arrayOf ( vararg elements : T ): Array < T > ( source ) fun main () { val numbers = arrayOf ( 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ) val names = arrayOf ( "Alice" , "Bob" , "Charlie" ) println ( numbers . joinToString ( ", " )) // 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 println ( names [ 0 ] ) // Alice } Source
docs.title: arrayOf

docs.text: arrayOfNulls Returns an array of objects of the given type with the given size, initialized with null values. expect fun < T > arrayOfNulls ( size : Int ): Array < T?> ( source ) // Create an array of 3 nullable Strings, all initialized to null val names : Array < String? > = arrayOfNulls ( 3 ) // Assign values to some elements names [ 0 ] = "Alice" names [ 2 ] = "Bob" // Print the array contents println ( names . contentToString ()) // Output: [Alice, null, Bob] Source
docs.title: arrayOfNulls

docs.text: Returns an array of objects of the given type with the given size, initialized with null values. expect fun < T > arrayOfNulls ( size : Int ): Array < T?> ( source ) // Create an array of 3 nullable Strings, all initialized to null val names : Array < String? > = arrayOfNulls ( 3 ) // Assign values to some elements names [ 0 ] = "Alice" names [ 2 ] = "Bob" // Print the array contents println ( names . contentToString ()) // Output: [Alice, null, Bob] Source
docs.title: arrayOfNulls

docs.text: assert Throws an AssertionError if the value is false and runtime assertions have been enabled on the JVM using the -ea JVM option. inline fun assert ( value : Boolean )( source ) fun main () { val a = 10 val b = 5 // Simple assertion using the inline assert with a lazy message assert ( a > b ) { "Expected a ( $ a ) to be greater than b ( $ b )" } } Source
docs.title: assert

docs.title: assert

docs.text: booleanArrayOf Returns an array containing the specified boolean values. expect fun booleanArrayOf ( vararg elements : Boolean ): BooleanArray ( source ) val flags = booleanArrayOf ( true , false , true ) println ( flags . size ) // prints 3 println ( flags [ 0 ] ) // prints true println ( flags [ 1 ] ) // prints false println ( flags [ 2 ] ) // prints true Source
docs.title: booleanArrayOf

docs.text: Returns an array containing the specified boolean values. expect fun booleanArrayOf ( vararg elements : Boolean ): BooleanArray ( source ) val flags = booleanArrayOf ( true , false , true ) println ( flags . size ) // prints 3 println ( flags [ 0 ] ) // prints true println ( flags [ 1 ] ) // prints false println ( flags [ 2 ] ) // prints true Source
docs.title: booleanArrayOf

docs.text: byteArrayOf Returns an array containing the specified Byte numbers. expect fun byteArrayOf ( vararg elements : Byte ): ByteArray ( source ) fun main () { val bytes = byteArrayOf ( 0 x01 , 0 x02 , 0 x03 , 0 x04 ) println ( bytes . joinToString ( prefix = "[" , postfix = "]" ) { "0x%02X" . format ( it ) }) } Source
